summary: No ship: R-9 is resolved, but R-8 remains incomplete and auto-merge is not retry-safe. Simpler alternative: keep the deterministic executor, but reconcile auto-merge as desired state. Open question: none.

findings.title: Blocker: auto-merge arming is not idempotent
findings.body: Trusted non-DIRTY PRs always become `skip`. If push and PR creation succeed but auto-merge arming fails or the process stops, the next poll finds the unarmed PR and skips forever. `pr-sweeper` only handles PRs already carrying `autoMergeRequest`, so an autoDeploy deployment silently stalls.
findings.recommendation: Query `autoMergeRequest` and treat `--auto-merge` as desired state: re-arm a trusted PR when missing, or fail with an actionable alert. Add a two-run witness covering PR-created/arming-failed/retry convergence, then re-pin RED.

findings.title: Blocker: the call-site gate can auto-merge approval PRs
findings.body: The plan moves auto-merge into the executor, but the committed gate merely finds a `--auto-merge` token anywhere in the workflow. Passing it unconditionally for both `bump` and `propose-pr` turns `autoDeploy:false` approval PRs into automatic deployments while every new witness can turn GREEN. That is a second observable behavior change outside the single flip.
findings.recommendation: Pin the policy mapping at the production seam: `bump` must invoke the executor with auto-merge and `propose-pr` without it. Prefer an explicit action argument interpreted by the executor, or add hermetic call-site witnesses for both lanes.

next_steps: Add retry-state and conditional auto-merge witnesses.
Re-pin red.sha and regenerate the machine-owned RED verify-record.
No further R-9 argv-ledger changes are required.
